methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Firestorm Labs is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector, including peers such as Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $60M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $120M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $150M to $29.0B. The wide range reflects material dispersion in the peer set, driven largely by Scale AI's $29.0B valuation relative to the $150M–$500M band occupied by Ares Industries, Vatn Systems, and Stark Defence.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $260M. Key uncertainties center on peer selection and comparability—specifically, whether Firestorm Labs' technology maturity, customer traction, and addressable market align more closely with the lower-valuation cohort ($150M–$500M) or with outliers like Scale AI. Additional clarity on revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and defensibility of IP would refine the range materially.
